title: The Constitution of India
simpleExplanation: The supreme law of India that lays down the framework defining fundamental political principles, procedures, powers, and duties of government institutions.
example: It guarantees the Right to Vote to every adult citizen under Article 326.
whyItMatters: It forms the foundation of our democratic rights and ensures the government operates within legal limits.

title: The Parliament (Lok Sabha & Rajya Sabha)
simpleExplanation: The supreme legislative body of India. It consists of the President and two houses: the Lok Sabha (House of the People) and the Rajya Sabha (Council of States).
example: Members of Parliament (MPs) debate and pass laws that apply to the entire country.
whyItMatters: It is where national laws are made and the Union Government is held accountable.

title: State Legislature (Vidhan Sabha)
simpleExplanation: The legislative body at the state level. Most states have a single house (Vidhan Sabha or Legislative Assembly).
example: Members of Legislative Assembly (MLAs) create laws related to state subjects like police, health, and agriculture.
whyItMatters: It manages governance and lawmaking for local issues within the state.

title: Election Commission of India (ECI)
simpleExplanation: An autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes in India at national and state levels.
example: The ECI prepares voter lists, schedules elections, and enforces the Model Code of Conduct.
whyItMatters: It ensures elections are conducted in a free, fair, and impartial manner.
